path: 01 Journal/2025/2025-09 September/Resources/2025-09-26 EPLAN - HARTING - AAS BasicEvents + MQTT/RP-AAS Notification Specification-260925-083241.pdf
text: E xported from E PLA Nwiki and for INTE RNA L USE ONLY Page 1 AAS Notification Specification Introduction The introduction of the Product Change Notifications (PCN – IDTA 02036-1-0) submodel means that we can now assume that new information about product changes will be announced regularly. This can be realized completely by AAS REST API. The aim of this specification is to define a notification mechanism through which changes to asset administration shells (AASs) and submodels in an AAS repository can be communicated to interested data consumers in near real time. This specification complements the by introducing a publish-subscribe (pub-sub) mechanism. While the REST API is designed to fulfil the purpose of providing standardized, structured access to data, the pub-sub mechanism complements this by enabling real-time notifications to data consumers. Specifically, it ensures that consumers are immediately informed of any changes to the data in the AAS repository, thereby enhancing responsiveness and eliminating the need for frequent polling/scraping of the repository to discover data updates. REST-type connections follow a request-response model. The client initiates communication by sending a request to the server, which then responds by sending back the requested data. This means that the client must actively poll the server to receive information. In contrast, MQTT publish-subscribe connections are event-driven. Clients subscribe to specific topics, and the broker automatically pushes updates to them whenever relevant data changes. MQTT Messaging MQTT was chosen as a lightweight, low-entry solution. It offers simple integration, minimal overhead, and is well-suited for event-driven communication without the complexity of a full streaming platform (like ). Topic Structure Within a topic, a sender can send a message to all topic subscribers. These will then receive the message. Authenticated users should each have a unique ID, e.g., the domain of a company. /noauth - public topic for messages that do not require authentication WIP - no official IDTA Specification AAS REST API Apache Kafka 1 /noauth 2 /all 3 /{receiverId} e.g. /eplan.com 4 /{receiverId}/{senderId} e.g. /eplan.com/phoenixcontact.com Exported from the EPLAN Knowledge Management System and for INTERNAL USE ONLY E xported from E PLA Nwiki and for INTE RNA L USE ONLY Page 2 /all - public topic for messages that require authentication with the broker /{receiverId} - private topic for messages to a specific authorized receiver /{receiverId}/{senderId} - private topic for messages from a specific authorized sender to a specific authorized receiver MQTT Payload The MQTT payload contains created / updated / deleted events of shells, submodels and submodel-elements. These events are based on for the meta-data of the event. Should the event be also available through another source (e.g. an event-REST-API), these meta- data must be the same as in the MQTT payload. This allows clients to identify an event via the id and to remember which event has already been processed. : Type: String Description: The version of the CloudEvents specification which the event uses. Constraints: REQUIRED MUST be a non-empty string Example: 1.0.0 Type: StringEnum - created, updated, deleted Description: This attribute contains a value describing the type of event related to the originating occurrence. Constraints: REQUIRED MUST be a non-empty string Type: URL-reference Description: Identifies the context in which an event happened. Depending on the , this is either the URI to the created / changed / deleted shell or submodel, or the URI to the BasicEventElement. It is intended that this URL can be used directly as endpoint by a further call to the REST API. Producers MUST ensure that source + id is unique for each distinct event. Constraints: REQUIRED MUST be a non-empty URI-reference The MQTT broker must enforce security. /all - only authenticated users are allowed to subscribe and post messages For consumer-specific topics, the broker must enforce that only the data provider and consumer with the respective id can post messages and subscribe to restricted topics: /{senderId} /{senderId}/{receiverId} MQTT messages should generally be send with a Quality of Service (QoS) level of at least 1 - deliver at least once, to ensure that messages reach every subscribed client. CloudEvents specversion type source Event Payload (data) Retrieval Mechanisms Exported from the EPLAN Knowledge Management System and for INTERNAL USE ONLY E xported from E PLA Nwiki and for INTE RNA L USE ONLY Page 3 An absolute URI is RECOMMENDED (required for to allow consumers to filter events) semanticId (for submodel / submodel-element) Type: URI-reference or IRDI Semantic ID of the Schema of the created / updated / deleted submodel, e.g., 0173-10029#01-XFB001#001 id Type: URI-reference Id of the created / updated / deleted shell or submodel Type: String Description: Identifies the event. Producers MUST ensure that id is unique for each distinct event. If a duplicate event is re-sent (e.g. due to a network error) it MUST have the same id . Consumers MAY assume that Events with identical id are duplicates. Constraints: REQUIRED MUST be a non-empty string MUST be unique within the scope of the producer Examples: A UUID Source + time Type: Timestamp Timestamp of when the occurrence happened. Type: String per Will probably be "application/json" Type: URI Description: Identifies the schema that a consumer will find when requesting source URI. Exampels: (optional) Type: JSON Object Description: The acutal payload of the event. Should only be transmitted via MQTT, if the payload is very small and the provider is certain that the payload (e.g. a created submodel) is of interest to the consumer. Otherwise, data should be an empty object an the consumer will decide to retrieve the payload via one of the . Example: subject Distribute Event Payloads (data) via direct Rest Api access: id time datacontenttype RFC 2046 dataschema https://api.swaggerhub.com/domains/Plattform_i40/Part1-MetaModel-Schemas/V3.1.0#/components/schemas/AssetAdministrationShell https://api.swaggerhub.com/domains/Plattform_i40/Part1-MetaModel-Schemas/V3.1.0#/components/schemas/Submodel https://api.swaggerhub.com/domains/Plattform_i40/Part1-MetaModel-Schemas/V3.1.0#/components/schemas/Property https://api.swaggerhub.com/domains/Plattform_i40/Part1-MetaModel-Schemas/V3.1.0#/components/schemas/BasicEventElement data Event Payload (data) Retrieval Mechanisms Exported from the EPLAN Knowledge Management System and for INTERNAL USE ONLY E xported from E PLA Nwiki and for INTE RNA L USE ONLY Page 4 {} Event Payload (data) Retrieval Mechanisms MQTT is designed to be a lightweight solution for the fast distribution of relatively small messages. Therefore, the imposes a maximum message size of 256 MB. Many brokers introduce a much smaller limit, to optimize performance (typically 128KB or 256KB). However, data models in AAS format, especially submodels, might exceed this limit and therefore need a mechanism to allow the data consumer to retrieve the payload after being notified of a change via MQTT with an empty data attribute. Method 1: Event payloads (data) via MQTT data attribute In the simplest case, the payload is small, so it does not exceed the broker's limit and can be transmitted directly in the MQTT message. Example MQTT message with update of a single submodel element in a submodel: Method 2: Event payloads (data) via AAS Rest-Api Allows clients to filter events and retrieve event payloads only if subject id and / or semantic id are relevant. Payload is requested as current state of shell / submodel / submodel-element. This method is easy to implement but may lead to clients missing changes if there are many changes in quick succession. Example MQTT message with plain event: source contains the URI of the created submodel dataschema points to the Submodel-schema subject contains submodel id and semantic ID MQTT Protocol Specification 1 { 2 "specversion": "1.0", 3 "type": "updated", 4 "source": "https://{serverUrl}/submodels/{base64Id}/submodel-elements/{idShortPath}", 5 "subject": { 6 "semanticId": "0173-10029#01-XFB001#001", 7 "id": "{submodelId}" 8 }, 9 "id": "{eventId} 10 "time": "2025-08-12T10:32:50.4369494Z", 11 "datacontenttype": "application/json", 12 "dataschema": "https://api.swaggerhub.com/domains/Plattform_i40/Part1-MetaModel-Schemas/V3.1.0#/components/schemas/Property", 13 "data": 14 { 15 "idShort": "PcnType", 16 "semanticId": { 17 "type": "ExternalReference", 18 "keys": [ 19 { 20 "type": "GlobalReference", 21 "value": "http://admin-shell.io/VDMA/Fluidics/ProductChangeNotification/PcnType/1/0" 22 } 23 ] 24 }, 25 "valueType": "xs:string", 26 "value": "PCD", 27 "valueId": { 28 "type": "ExternalReference", 29 "keys": [ 30 { 31 "type": "GlobalReference", 32 "value": "0173-10029#07-ABO119#001" 33 } 34 ] 35 }, 36 "modelType": "Property" 37 } 38 } 39 Exported from the EPLAN Knowledge Management System and for INTERNAL USE ONLY E xported from E PLA Nwiki and for INTE RNA L USE ONLY Page 5 Method 3: Event payloads (data) via BasicEventElement Uses BasicEventElement to deliver actual events in paginated batches. MQTT notification notifies the data consumer about availability of new events in the BasicEventElement. Example MQTT message with BasicEventElement: source contains the URI of the BasicEventElement dataschema points to the BasicEventElement-schema no subject HTTP GET Request: {event.source}?time={event.time}&include=data BasicEventElement REST response example of a created submodel and updated submodel element. “Deleted” is a special case because the related element may not exist anymore in an AAS servers database. This is especially important, because ?time= may be any past time and is different for each client. 1 { 2 "specversion": "1.0", 3 "type": "created", 4 "source": 1) identify the deleted object directly by id/idShortPath, 2) identify the object in which a child object was deleted, so that a client may need to update by an additional API call 3) identify the object in which a child object was deleted and provide a notDeleted list, so that the client may update internally without further information. 1 { 2 // additional meta-data 3 // ... 4 "specversion":
